messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "avl-framework-qualification-forecloses-large-operator-cell",
 "title": "Buyer-side procurement norms (AVL qualification, ISO 27001/IEC 62443 attestations, global frameworks) close the chosen 40% large-operator beachhead for the entire Year-1 window",
 "narrative": "The venture's stated beachhead is large operators — modeled as 40% of PDU spend. But that cell is not entered by winning a bake-off; it is entered by surviving an approved-vendor-list qualification: vendor security questionnaires demanding ISO 27001 and secure-development (IEC 62443-4-1) attestations, TÜV/CB-scheme type-test reports, factory audits, and financial-viability checks — a 12–18 month cycle that no law mandates and no market report prices. On top of that, the corpus shows the cell's spend is increasingly channelled through multi-year global frameworks (Digital Realty's $373M Schneider agreement, Iren's all-in Dell contract) and integrator bundles, meaning much of the nominal 0.40 is never tendered at PDU line-item level at all. The 0.40 value is an unsourced internal estimate; the reachable-in-window fraction of it is far smaller. The model books the beachhead as the largest cell; procurement norms make it the slowest.",
 "category": "competitive",
 "targetNodes": [
  "cust.operator-large",
  "obtainableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Large-operator procurement requires AVL qualification artifacts (ISO 27001 / secure-development attestations, TÜV/CB reports, audits) that a pre-launch entrant does not yet hold — a buyer-side norm, not a legal instrument, so it appears on no regulatory checklist",
   "refs": [
    "profile:Company",
    "ledger:cust.operator-large",
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c13"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The spend that does move moves through global frameworks and integrator bundles rather than contestable PDU line items — the corpus documents Digital Realty/Schneider, Iren/Dell and Jülich-style bundling — so even qualified challengers see few discrete PDU decisions in a 12-month window",
   "refs": [
    "digest:refine-structure-independence.global-framework-forecloses-large-operator-cell#c0",
    "digest:refine-boundary-substitution.large-operator-channel-forecloses-to-odm#c0",
    "digest:cust-mix#c0"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The contestable large-operator cell is materially below 0.40 and the Year-1 pace within it approaches zero; since this is the declared primary channel, the YAM is effectively being earned from the other 60% of the buyer mix",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:cust.operator-large",
    "ledger:obtainableFactor",
    "profile:Product"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The follow-up answer 'we sell to large operators first' is recorded as a strategy choice, and the 0.40 cell makes it look like the biggest pond. AVL cycle time and framework lock-up are procurement plumbing — invisible in every market report the model cites (the channel split is admitted to be entirely unsourced), so nothing in the ledger contradicts the comfortable reading.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "Two CE large operators confirming a fast-track vendor-qualification path (<6 months) for PDU suppliers and at least one 2026 PDU tender from a large CE operator that is open to non-AVL vendors.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.45,
  "rationale": "Framework/bundled procurement at large operators is directly evidenced in the corpus (named contracts), and 12–18 month AVL cycles are standard for critical-power vendors; uncertainty is whether specific CE colos run lighter qualification for rack-level gear.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "cust.operator-large",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.25,
   "note": "Framework- and bundle-locked spend removed: contestable direct large-operator share for a new entrant"
  },
  {
   "nodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.007,
   "note": "Year-1 pace re-weighted toward slower-to-qualify channels"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "AVL qualification requirements and cycle times quoted in operator supplier-onboarding portals",
   "where": "Equinix/Digital Realty/NTT GDC supplier registration portals; Vantage/CyrusOne EU procurement contacts",
   "threshold": "Mandatory ISO 27001 or 12+ month qualification stated for electrical-fit-out vendors",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Share of PDU spend announced inside multi-year framework or integrated-rack deals",
   "where": "Operator capex calls and press releases (Digital Realty, Iren-class contracts); Schneider/Vertiv earnings commentary on framework backlog",
   "threshold": "≥1 new CE operator announcing a global power-train framework covering rack PDUs",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Run three structured CE operator/distributor procurement interviews focused specifically on AVL cycle time and framework coverage of PDU line items",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "cust.operator-large",
   "note": "This is also the ledger's own promotionPath for the whole customer dimension"
  },
  {
   "action": "Enter the large-operator cell through an already-qualified integrator/OEM as channel (white-label or co-brand) rather than direct AVL qualification",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Trades margin for window; converts a 15-month gate into a purchase order"
  },
  {
   "action": "Start ISO 27001 / secure-development certification now, in parallel with CRA artifacts, since the same evidence serves both the legal and procurement gates",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  }
 ],
 "lens": "regulatory-gauntlet",
 "riskId": "risk.regulatory-gauntlet.avl-framework-qualification-forecloses-large-operator-cell"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).
